data.title: Kimbell Royalty Partners reports mixed Q2 results; reaffirms FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Kimbell Royalty Partners press release (NYSE:KRP): Q2 GAAP EPS of $0.02 misses by $0.10.
  • Revenue of $86.55M (+13.0% Y/Y) beats by $4.9M.
  • Total second quarter 2025 consolidated Adjusted EBITDA was $63.8 million (consolidated Adjusted EBITDA is a non-GAAP financial measure.
  • Second quarter 2025 run-rate average daily production was 25,355 Boe per day (6:1), which was composed of approximately 47% from natural gas (6:1) and approximately 53% from liquids (33% from oil and 20% from NGLs).
  • Kimbell affirms its financial and operational guidance ranges for 2025 previously disclosed in its Q4 2024 earnings release.

data.title: Astronics raises 2025 revenue guidance to $840M–$860M as aerospace momentum accelerates
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Astronics Corporation (ATRO) Q2 2025

Management View

  • Peter J. Gundermann, Chairman, President and CEO, emphasized "consistent progress towards improved performance for the business," highlighting sales just under $205 million, a 3.3% increase from the prior year and similar to the first quarter. He noted "record sales for our Aerospace segment, which compensated for lower Test segment sales." The company made a $6.4 million adjustment in the Test segment and took $6.2 million in restructuring charges by exiting noncore aerospace product lines. Gundermann explained, "These product lines have proven to be low growth and low margin and the charges relate to write-downs of inventory on certain facility assets."
  • Gundermann also announced the acquisition of Envoy Aerospace, stating, "We believe that having an ODA will be a competitive differentiator as we are often involved in aircraft retrofit programs and FAA certification is becoming a more important capability in competitive situations."
  • Nancy L. Hedges, CFO, reported that "revenue in the second quarter grew 3% over the prior year period and was in line with the trailing first quarter," despite the $6.4 million Test segment impact. She added, "Adjusted EBITDA was $25.4 million or 12.4% of sales, up from 10.2% last year, primarily reflecting improved profitability from higher volume and increasing productivity in the Aerospace segment."
  • Hedges noted, "GAAP earnings per share was unchanged year-over-year at $0.04. Non-GAAP adjusted EPS for the quarter was $0.38, nearly double the $0.20 from the prior year period."

Outlook

  • Gundermann stated, "Given all this, we are increasing our 2025 revenue guide to $840 million to $860 million, up from $820 million to $860 million, an increase of $10 million at the midpoint." He added, "We expect the third quarter to be up marginally from the pace of the first half with the fourth quarter up more substantially."
  • Management expects Aerospace to continue benefiting from "increasing passenger traffic, aircraft utilization, aircraft build rates and the increasing adoption of modern passenger entertainment and connectivity systems."
  • The company remains "optimistic that we will be able to create value in the emerging LEO world as we have in the past with GEO and ATG."

Financial Results

  • Year-to-date sales reached $411 million, up 7.2% from last year. Aerospace segment sales set a quarterly record at $193.6 million, a 9.4% increase year-over-year, with commercial transport sales up 13% and military sales up 11%.
  • Hedges reported, "On an adjusted basis, gross margin expanded 120 basis points over the prior year to 29.2% and operating margin expanded 250 basis points to 8.9%."
  • Adjusted Aerospace operating profit was $31.5 million, up from $23.5 million a year ago. Adjusted operating margin improved by 300 basis points year-over-year to 16.3% in Aerospace.
  • The Test segment saw sales of $11.1 million and an adjusted operating loss of $6.7 million, impacted by the EAC adjustment. Hedges noted, "We expect these benefits will be more visible in the second half of the year."
  • The company used $7.6 million in cash from operations, impacted by $21.6 million in payments related to the U.K. patent dispute and $12 million in income tax payments. Total liquidity at quarter end was about $191 million.
  • Capital expenditures for the quarter were $4.6 million, with full-year CapEx expected in the range of $40 million to $50 million.

Q&A

  • Jonathan E. Tanwanteng, CJS Securities: Asked about drivers of aerospace momentum and the outlook for Test. Gundermann explained, "Production rates, first of all, are a pretty big driver. We've got 737 rates going up, A320, there's upward pressure... we are on a glide path to step up again to like high 30s and even up to the low 40s potentially by the end of the year if Boeing gets permission to go there." He also noted the forthcoming Airbus A220 program and military growth.
  • Tanwanteng inquired about margin outlook and tariffs. Gundermann replied, "The tariff situation, I feel is still a little bit hard to predict... pricing increases that we've negotiated into certain of our contracts are also helping us quite a bit on the margin front."
  • Michael Frank Ciarmoli, Truist Securities: Probed on sustaining 16% adjusted margin in Aerospace. Gundermann responded, "I think we're pretty comfortable with it... we continue to be successful" with pricing actions.
  • Ciarmoli asked about aftermarket trends. Gundermann stated, "The retrofit side continues to be a very positive place for us also... we're continuing to see pretty strong opportunities there."
  • On Test segment strategy, Gundermann said, "We don't have any immediate actions in place along those lines. Frankly, we've got our head down trying to get through this difficult patch that we're in."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts focused on sustainability of Aerospace margins, tariff impacts, aftermarket trends, and the strategic future of the Test segment, often prompting detailed management explanations.
  • Management maintained a confident tone during prepared remarks, emphasizing "continued improvement" and "strong tailwinds." During Q&A, Gundermann acknowledged uncertainties, especially regarding tariffs and Test, while expressing optimism about mitigating actions and margin improvements.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, analysts' tone was more probing on structural changes and margin sustainability, while management was slightly more defensive but continued to express confidence in core Aerospace performance and improvement efforts in Test.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The company increased its 2025 revenue guidance by $10 million at the midpoint, compared to holding steady guidance in the previous quarter.
  • Strategic actions this quarter included the exit from noncore Aerospace product lines, a step not announced in Q1.
  • Test segment challenges deepened with a larger EAC adjustment and higher operating loss, while Aerospace continued to set new sales records.
  • Management tone shifted from routine operational confidence in Q1 to a more nuanced approach, addressing both significant progress and the impact of unusual adjustments in Q2.
  • Analysts in Q2 pressed more on margin sustainability, tariffs, and strategic reviews, while Q1 questions centered on demand sources and risk mitigation.

Risks and Concerns

  • Significant risks cited included ongoing tariff impacts, with costs estimated at $15 million to $20 million and mitigation efforts underway.
  • The Test segment remains challenged with EAC-related adjustments and continued operating losses.
  • Patent litigation in the U.K. resulted in a $3.5 million legal fee reimbursement, though this is partially offset by reduced legal and R&D expenses.
  • Management is monitoring supply chain shifts, implementing cost control measures, and evaluating further actions to address risks in both the Test and Aerospace segments.

Final Takeaway

Management underscored strong momentum in the core Aerospace segment, record sales growth, and a raised revenue outlook for 2025, while candidly addressing ongoing challenges in the Test segment and tariff impacts. Actions to simplify the portfolio, strengthen operational systems, and pursue strategic acquisitions like Envoy Aerospace aim to position the company for continued growth and improved profitability through the second half of the year and into 2026.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Granite Construction reports mixed Q2 results; updates FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Granite Construction press release (NYSE:GVA): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.93 beats by $0.23.
  • Revenue of $1.13B (+2.7% Y/Y) misses by $30M.
  • Adjusted EBITDA (2) increased $22 million to $152 million compared to $130 million for the same period in the prior year.

  • Outlook

    With the acquisitions announced this week, we are updating our 2025 fiscal year guidance as noted below:

    • Revenue in the range of $4.35 billion to $4.55 billion with revenue from the new acquisitions of approximately $150 million (prior $4.2 billion to $4.4 billion) vs. consensus of $4.30B
    • Adjusted EBITDA margin increased to a range of 11.25% to 12.25%
    • SG&A expense unchanged at approximately 9.0% of revenue, inclusive of an estimated $40 million of stock-based compensation expense
    • Effective tax rate for adjusted net income unchanged in the Mid-20s
    • Capital expenditures unchanged with a range of $140 million to $160 million
